# Introduction {#sec-intro}

This chapter establishes the problem and motivation. A good first chapter does three things:

1. States the central question the book answers, in one sentence the reader can repeat.
2. Explains why that question is hard — what makes naive approaches fail.
3. Tours the rest of the book, noting which chapters depend on which.

## Why this topic matters

Open with a concrete scenario, not an abstract definition. A reader who closes the book here should still understand the stakes.

## What you'll learn

State the deliverable explicitly. By the end of this book, the reader will be able to:

- Capability one
- Capability two
- Capability three

::: {.aside}
The `aside` div renders as a kaobook sidenote in the wide outer margin. Use
asides for tangential commentary that should live alongside the body but not
interrupt the main flow.
:::

## Roadmap

Map each chapter to what it teaches:

| Chapter | Topic | Depends on |
|---|---|---|
| @sec-foundations | Foundations | — |
| (next) | Applications I | foundations |
| (next) | Applications II | foundations, applications I |

References work via Quarto's cross-ref system. To cite a paper: [@bjork1992].
The bibliography lives in `references.bib`; add entries with
`beagle-latex:latex-bibliography`.

## Notation

Establish notation here, once. Subsequent chapters reference back rather than re-introducing symbols.

| Symbol | Meaning |
|---|---|
| $\mathcal{X}$ | Input space |
| $\mathcal{Y}$ | Output space |
| $f_\theta$ | Model parameterized by $\theta$ |
| $\mathcal{D}$ | Dataset |
